theme: Studying Alone vs. Studying in a Group

sentences.english: Some students prefer to study alone.
sentences.korean: 일부 학생들은 혼자 공부하는 것을 선호한다.

sentences.english: Others prefer to study in a group.
sentences.korean: 다른 학생들은 그룹으로 공부하는 것을 선호한다.

sentences.english: Which do you prefer and why?

sentences.korean: 개인적으로, 저는 혼자 공부하는 것을 선호해요.

sentences.english: When I study by myself, I can move at my own pace without having to wait for others or adjust to a group schedule.
sentences.korean: 혼자 공부하면, 다른 사람을 기다리거나 그룹 일정에 맞출 필요 없이 제 속도로 진행할 수 있어요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Studying Alone vs. Studying in a Group

theme: Studying Alone vs. Studying in a Group

sentences.english: I also tend to get distracted very easily when other people are talking, even if the conversation is related to the subject.
sentences.korean: 다른 사람들이 말하면 아주 쉽게 산만해지는 편이에요, 대화가 주제와 관련이 있더라도요.

sentences.english: Studying alone allows me to stay fully focused.
sentences.korean: 혼자 공부하면 완전히 집중할 수 있어요.

sentences.english: That said, I do think group study has benefits — for example, it can be helpful when preparing for discussions or when you're stuck on a problem.
sentences.korean: 그렇긴 하지만, 그룹 학습에도 장점이 있다고 생각해요 — 예를 들어, 토론을 준비하거나 문제에 막혔을 때 도움이 될 수 있어요.

sentences.english: But for my daily study routine, I find individual study to be more efficient and productive.
sentences.korean: 하지만 일상적인 공부 루틴에서는 혼자 공부하는 것이 더 효율적이고 생산적이라고 느껴요.

sentences.english: I retain information better when I read and take notes quietly on my own.
sentences.korean: 혼자 조용히 읽고 필기할 때 정보를 더 잘 기억해요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Learning from Older vs. Younger People
Studying Alone vs. Studying in a Group
